reasoning_content: We need to check if 7897 is prime. Quick sanity check: check divisibility by small primes. First, note that 7897 is odd, not divisible by 2. Sum of digits: 7+8+9+7=31, not divisible by 3, so not divisible by 3. Last digit not 0 or 5, so not divisible by 5. Check mod 7: 7*1128=7896, remainder 1, so not divisible by 7. Check mod 11: alternating sum: 7-8+9-7=1, not divisible by 11. Check mod 13: 13*607=7891, remainder 6; 13*608=7904, too big. So not divisible by 13. Check mod 17: 17*464=7888, remainder 9; 17*465=7905, so no. Check mod 19: 19*415=7885, remainder 12; 19*416=7904, so no. Check mod 23: 23*343=7889, remainder 8; 23*344=7912, no. Check mod 29: 29*272=7888, remainder 9; 29*273=7917, no. Check mod 31: 31*254=7874, remainder 23; 31*255=7905, no. Check mod 37: 37*213=7881, remainder 16; 37*214=7918, no. Check mod 41: 41*192=7872, remainder 25; 41*193=7913, no. Check mod 43: 43*183=7869, remainder 28; 43*184=7912, no. Check mod 47: 47*168=7896, remainder 1, so not divisible. Check mod 53: 53*149=7897? Actually 53*149 = 53*150 - 53 = 7950 - 53 = 7897? Let's compute: 53*149 = 53*(100+49)=5300+2597=7897. Yes! So 7897 = 53 * 149. Both are primes? 53 is prime, 149 is prime (since 149 mod 2,3,5,7,11,13: 149/7=21.285, 7*21=147 remainder 2; 11*13=143 remainder 6; 13*11=143; 13*12=156; so prime). So 7897 is composite. Thus answer: not prime.
